# Fixture corpus вЂ” PocketScribe

Captured FilePlugin output for decoder golden tests and the full-stack integration
pipeline. Every fixture triplet consists of:

- `block-{H}-meta` вЂ” length-delimited `RequestFinalizeBlock` + `ResponseFinalizeBlock`
  protobuf records, exactly as the Cosmos SDK FilePlugin writes them.
- `block-{H}-data` вЂ” uvarint-delimited `StoreKVPairs` records (supplier + other KV stores).
- `block-{H}-expected.json` вЂ” generated by `go run ./tools/fixtureextract <H> <dir>`;
  used by `TestGoldenWalkAllFixtures` to verify the live decode pipeline is stable.

## Era-dir convention

The **directory name = the decoder version that must decode that block**, following
the break map (`docs/research/supplier-shape-breaks.md`). The router's lenient
fallback maps any upgrade version that has no registered decoder to the nearest
earlier registered one, which the break map proves is shape-safe.

| Registered decoder | Era covers |
|---|---|
| `v0_1_0` | genesis through v0.1.7-era (heights 1вЂ“78670) |
| `v0_1_8` | v0.1.8вЂ“v0.1.9-era (heights 78671вЂ“78682) |
| `v0_1_10` | v0.1.10вЂ“v0.1.19-era (heights 78683вЂ“135296) |
| `v0_1_20` | v0.1.20вЂ“v0.1.26-era (heights 135297вЂ“247892) |
| `v0_1_27` | v0.1.27-era (heights 247893вЂ“287931) |
| `v0_1_28` | v0.1.28-era (heights 287932вЂ“382249) |
| `v0_1_29` | v0.1.29-era (heights 382250вЂ“484472) |
| `v0_1_30` | v0.1.30+ (heights 484473+; v0.1.31, v0.1.33 fall back here) |

A fixture that lives in `v0_1_10/` may have been captured during the v0.1.17 binary
era (e.g. block 102542) вЂ” the directory reflects the _decoder_, not the poktroll
binary version. Provenance (which binary era produced each block) is captured in
the matrix below.

## Fixture selection criteria (spec В§8.1)

Per version, ~3 fixtures are selected:
1. **Boundary block** (`applied_height` вЂ” first block under the new binary).
2. **Max-activity block** вЂ” highest supplier activity (msg_stake, events, KV). In eras
   with zero supplier activity (expected for the 5вЂ“13-block v0.1.2вЂ“v0.1.9 eras),
   use the tx-bearing block as a negative fixture (documents absence of supplier
   activity, mirrors the v0_1_0 precedent at heights 1вЂ“3).
3. **Quiet block** (tx_count == 0, no supplier KV). When the boundary block itself
   is quiet, it satisfies both criteria 1 and 3 (deduped to 2 fixtures for short eras).

## Coverage matrix

> Status key: **covered** = triplet(s) present; **negative-only** = only quiet/tx-bearing blocks, zero supplier activity (valid coverage for short eras); **pending curation (batch N)** = tarball downloaded, curation not yet run; **PENDING вЂ” no archived data** = archeology still running.
